core_concept: Human Body Systems: Skeletal, Muscular, Respiratory, Circulatory, Nervous
facts: Bones are hard and give your body shape; without bones you would be floppy like a jellyfish.
Muscles pull on bones to make your arms and legs move; when you bend your arm, a muscle on the front gets shorter.
Your heart is a pump that pushes blood through your body; you can feel it beat faster when you run.
Your lungs take in air when you breathe; they are like balloons that fill up and then let air out.
Your brain is the boss that sends messages through nerves to tell your muscles what to do.
misconceptions: Muscles push bones to make them move, like pushing a toy car.
The heart is shaped like a valentine and its job is to make you feel love.
